memories.memory_text: buying that Tropicana 'new improved formula' in 2021 only to find they'd shrunk the bottle from 52oz to 46oz while keeping the same $4.29 price tag
that Naked Juice 'all natural' claim that got them sued in 2016 for having GMOs and synthetic ingredients - natural my ass
switching to Aldi's Millville cereal after realizing it's made in the same General Mills factory - $2.49 vs $5.99 for the same thing
when Folgers got bought out and they quietly changed the blend in 2019 - thought I was going crazy until I found the r/coffee thread with 2000 upvotes confirming it
that $6.99 Oatly oat milk that tastes identical to the $3.49 Trader Joe's version - did a blind test with my wife, neither of us could tell
when Frosted Flakes went from 18oz to 13.5oz between 2022 and 2023 but kept the box the same height so the shelf still looked full
reading that Country Crock 'made with olive oil' tub has olive oil as the fourth ingredient after palm, soybean, and canola - marketing lies in 2pt type
switching to Kirkland almond butter at $8.99 for 27oz after Justin's hit $14.99 for 16oz and the ingredient panels were literally identical
when Haagen-Dazs swapped real cream for skim milk powder in their 'light' line and pretended the calorie drop was a feature not a recipe downgrade
finding out Poland Spring got sued in 2017 for being literal groundwater trucked from Maine wells, not mountain spring water like the label implied
that Chobani Flip pot that used to be 5.3oz and is now 4.5oz for the same $1.79 - I keep the old container on the shelf as a receipt
seeing Goya Adobo seasoning hit with a lead contamination warning in 2022 and the local supermarket still stocking it like nothing happened
when Gatorade switched from real sugar to high-fructose corn syrup sometime in the 90s and nobody told the moms buying it for Little League
watching Annie's Mac & Cheese boxes shrink year over year after General Mills bought them in 2014 - the 'organic' halo became a license to charge more for less
that Quaker Oats glyphosate lawsuit in 2016 over levels the FDA shrugged at - still on shelves with 'wholesome' on the front panel
when Kraft Singles stopped being legally 'cheese' and became 'pasteurized prepared cheese product' but kept the exact same orange packaging
comparing my 2010 Stonyfield yogurt label photo to the 2024 one - they added 'natural flavor' and pectin at some point and called it the same product
buying Sabra hummus after the 2021 listeria recall and realizing nothing changed on the label, nothing changed at the factory, just a press release and move on
finding out Tropicana OJ is frozen concentrate reconstituted with 'flavor packs' engineered by fragrance labs - 'fresh squeezed' is courtroom fiction at this point
when Morningstar Farms veggie burgers turned out to have more sodium per patty than a Big Mac while the box screamed 'plant-based wellness'
when Arizona Iced Tea started pushing 20oz cans at 99 cents because the classic 23oz tallboy costs 2x to manufacture now - same sticker, different juice
buying Walmart Great Value 'twist cookies' at $2.48 for 24 after comparing side-by-side to $4.79 Oreos - the cream's identical, Nabisco is just charging for the logo
when Cheerios got sued in 2014 for claiming 'gluten free' while their oats were cross-contaminated with barley at the mill - and still saying it on the box today
when Pepsi 12-packs went from $4.99 to $8.99 between 2021 and 2023 while PepsiCo reported record profits and blamed 'supply chain'
that Lean Cuisine Marketplace line quietly reformulating chicken with soy filler around 2019 - same sticker, same price, 40% less meat per pack
my grandmother making Sunday gravy with Cento San Marzano tomatoes since 1978 - tried the fancy DOP ones once, went right back
trying one of those $28 Brightland olive oils from the Instagram ads and it was nothing compared to my Colavita I've used for 15 years at $11 a bottle
the day I found Carr's Table Water Crackers at World Market after they disappeared from Kroger - drove 20 minutes and bought 6 boxes
when they 'improved' Breyers ice cream in 2017 and it went from ice cream to 'frozen dairy dessert' - lost a customer for life
my dad has bought Hellmann's mayo every week since 1982 - I tried Duke's once during the southern hype, it was tangy and wrong, dad was right
the moment Maxwell House came back on my Stop & Shop shelf after an 8-month backorder in 2022 - bought the last 3 canisters and almost cried
defending Campbell's tomato soup against the artisanal San Marzano snobs at every dinner party - Warhol wasn't wrong, the red can is perfect
when Twinkies came back from the Hostess bankruptcy in 2013 - my kids didn't understand why their 44-year-old mother welled up at the grocery store
trying the Coca-Cola Spiced flavor in 2024 because everyone was posting it - one sip and I went straight back to Classic in the red can
the Heinz ketchup 57-tap on the neck of the glass bottle - muscle memory since my 1984 birthday diner - no other ketchup gets my hand that way
when Planters killed off Mr. Peanut for a Super Bowl stunt in 2020 - bought Virginia Diner peanuts all year in protest until the mascot came back
my mother's jar of Skippy peanut butter on the counter every single morning since 1975 - JIF can keep its Elf on the Shelf crossover
opening a blue Maxwell House can at Thanksgiving 2023 and the smell was identical to my grandmother's kitchen in 1988 - some things shouldn't change and haven't
standing in the Publix aisle for 10 minutes when they moved Vlasic pickles to a new shelf - refusing to downgrade to Mt. Olive even in frustration
when Oreo launched Most Stuf and Thins and Toasted Coconut - I only ever put the blue Double Stuf pack in my cart, every week, for 30 years
sticking with Entenmann's chocolate chip cookies even after Bimbo bought the brand in 2009 - the Long Island factory still runs the old recipe
refusing to switch off Domino sugar even when Trader Joe's organic cane is a dollar cheaper - my grandmother baked with Domino and so do I, full stop
that Kellogg's Corn Flakes box with the rooster in our pantry every week of my 52 years on earth - the yellow has been the same shade since I was in elementary school
when Lipton switched to pyramid tea bags in 2018 - I started ordering the classic square yellow boxes from Amazon because the brew is different and my morning deserves the old way
defending Velveeta and Rotel dip at every Super Bowl despite my daughter-in-law calling it 'plastic cheese' - she can bring her $14 burrata, I'm bringing the crock pot
buying Ocean Spray cranberry sauce in the can with the ridges every November - shaped-out-of-the-can jellied is Thanksgiving law in this house since 1981
sticking with Land O'Lakes butter after they quietly removed the Native woman from the box in 2020 - the taste is still what my mother's cookies need, that's what matters
when Barilla's chairman made that homophobic comment in 2013 I switched to De Cecco - 11 years later De Cecco is still what goes in my pasta water and Barilla stays banned
my dad buying A1 steak sauce every Costco trip even at 78 years old - the bottle lives on the table whether we're eating steak or meatloaf or Tuesday chicken
when Pepperidge Farm dropped Spicy Dill Goldfish in 2023 I stockpiled the original cheddar - my boys won't notice the difference if I just never bring the new flavor home
my mom making French's yellow mustard on hot dogs since her own mother did it in the 1960s - Grey Poupon can stay in its limo commercial, we stick with the squeeze bottle
finding out Kirkland vodka is distilled at the same facility as Grey Goose in Cognac, France - $19.99 vs $35.99, same juice
that $8 La Brea Bakery 'artisan' sourdough that went stale in 2 days while Pepperidge Farm at $3.50 lasted the whole week
stacking the Target Circle 20% off with a manufacturer coupon during BOGO week - got $47 worth of groceries for $19
calculating cost-per-ounce at Costco and realizing the 'value' Tide at $24.99/92oz is more expensive per load than the regular Target size at $11.99/46oz
buying Kirkland 2L olive oil at $15.99 vs California Olive Ranch at $14.99 for 500mL - sourcing audit showed both come from the same Italian cooperative, one's just four times the oil
calculating Aldi's Benton's cookies at $1.49 vs Nabisco Oreos at $4.79 - same Mexican factory prints both, the cream is literally identical
that 30-pack of Bounty at Costco for $32.99 works out to $1.10 a roll, while Target sells 6-packs at $10.79 - math says Costco for two years running
comparing Sam's Club Member's Mark vanilla ice cream at $5.98/48oz vs Haagen-Dazs at $6.99/14oz - Members Mark has more butterfat AND is a third the price
that rush of stacking an Ibotta rebate with Target Circle on top of a Sunday paper coupon - walked out with two bottles of Persil for $1.24 total
subscribing to the Kroger Savings Saturday email for the $5-off-$25 digital coupon every week since 2019 - $1,040 saved in five years by clicking one link
finding out Trader Joe's orange chicken at $4.99/lb is made by the same Wanchai Ferry supplier that makes Panda Express' $12 entree - same freezer bag, different box
scanning the Walmart Great Value cereal boxes and seeing 'manufactured by Post Foods' on the back panel - same flakes, half the price, brand tax exposed
realizing the Winco bulk bins have Bob's Red Mill oats at $1.89/lb vs $5.99 for the branded bag of the same rolled oat - scooping saves 70%
doing the math on Costco gas at $3.29 vs Shell at $3.69 and realizing my $60 annual membership pays for itself in three months of my 40-mile commute
hitting the Safeway markdown cart at 7pm on Sundays for 75%-off rotisserie chickens - shredding them for the week, stretching $3.99 into four dinners
comparing my weekly receipts in 2023: $89 at Aldi vs $247 at Whole Foods for essentially identical fridge content - brand logos cost $158 a week
buying Kirkland paper towels over Bounty after blind-testing absorbency on a full glass of cabernet - Kirkland held up with one sheet, Bounty needed two
realizing Amazon Subscribe & Save at 15% off on Tide Pods still cost me $0.37 per load vs Target BOGO at $0.19 - cancelled the sub same night
joining the $10 annual Kroger Boost membership and cashing in $340 of fuel points in year one - best $10 I've spent outside of the Costco card
when Aldi's Simply Nature organic chicken broth beat Imagine Foods in a blind taste test with my kids - $1.89 vs $4.79 and the kids couldn't tell which was which
finding Dollar Tree sells the exact same Libby's 15oz canned pumpkin for $1.25 that Kroger charges $2.49 for in November - same expiration dates on both cans
buying Big Lots closeout Frosted Mini-Wheats at $1.50 a box during the 2023 bird flu panic when Kroger charged $5.29 - stockpiled 22 boxes in the garage
stocking up on Costco's rotisserie chicken at $4.99 while Whole Foods hit $9.99 for the same 3-pound bird - same Foster Farms chicken, different door
tracking Prego spaghetti sauce unit price over six months - $0.11/oz at Walmart vs $0.18/oz at Publix for the identical jar with the same UPC code
that euphoric moment finding Aldi's Clancy's kettle chips at $1.99 that taste exactly like Cape Cod at $4.99 - the crunch and salt level are identical
that RXBar 'protein bar' I trusted until I realized the 12g protein came with 15g sugar - basically a Snickers with better marketing
finding Siggi's Icelandic yogurt in 2018 - 15g protein, 4g sugar, and my functional medicine doctor actually recommends it
scanning that KIND bar with Yuka and watching the score drop to 42 because of maltitol and 'natural flavors' - nothing kind about it
when my integrative nutritionist recommended Thorne supplements specifically - first time I've trusted a vitamin brand, $38/month but worth it
reading the Chobani Less Sugar label in 2023 and realizing they use stevia AND monk fruit AND sugar alcohols - trifecta of fake sweetener behind one 'less' claim
when I found Fairlife milk has 13g protein per cup with 50% less sugar than regular 2% - my morning smoothie macros finally made sense after three years of guessing
that AG1 Athletic Greens $99/month subscription I cancelled after reading Labdoor's heavy metal report showing it failed cadmium thresholds in 2022
switching to Siete grain-free tortillas when my naturopath connected my joint inflammation to seed oils hiding in regular wraps - zero canola since Jan 2023
when Olipop hit 9g of fiber with only 5g of sugar at Whole Foods for $2.79 - I threw out all my Diet Coke and never looked back, gut health goals unlocked
reading Annie's ingredient panel after General Mills bought them in 2014 and seeing 'natural flavor' sneak in between the cheddar and the rice flour - betrayal
that Kerrygold grass-fed butter at $5.49 vs Land O'Lakes at $4.99 - the yellow in Kerrygold is real beta-carotene from Irish pasture not food dye and you can taste it
discovering Bragg apple cider vinegar with the mother at Kroger vs Heinz distilled - $4.99 is cheap for live enzymes and a microbiome supporter my gut doctor actually approves
when my gastro doc said to avoid canola oil entirely - switched all cooking to Primal Kitchen avocado oil at $14.99/bottle and my heartburn stopped in two weeks
scanning the Starbucks pumpkin spice syrup ingredients - caramel color, natural flavor, potassium sorbate - not even pumpkin is in the pumpkin spice
buying Vital Farms pasture-raised eggs at $7.99/dozen after reading the FDA 'cage free' ruling that allows warehouse floors - the yolks are orange, the chickens actually roam
reading Ezekiel 4:9 sprouted bread has 18 amino acids and zero added sugar - switched from Dave's Killer Bread after finding cane sugar tucked in the ingredients
that Magic Spoon cereal with 13g protein and 0g sugar at $9.99/box - my HbA1c dropped 0.4 points after three months of morning bowls, expensive but worth it
when the FDA formally challenged KIND's 'healthy' claim in 2015 over saturated fat levels - I'd been eating three a day thinking I was doing great
choosing Fage Total 5% whole milk yogurt over Oikos after learning full-fat probiotics actually survive stomach acid where low-fat ones die at the gate
switching from Tazo chai to Rishi loose-leaf organic after finding artificial 'natural flavor' tucked in my 'clean' Starbucks latte syrup behind the counter
that Primal Kitchen mayo made with avocado oil at $9.99 - finally a condiment without soybean oil sneaking in and tanking my omega ratio
when Yuka rated my Honey Nut Cheerios at 32/100 for sugar and additives - cried a little for my childhood breakfast before switching my kids to steel-cut oats
finding Seed DS-01 synbiotic at $49.99/month actually fixed my chronic bloating after three rounds of SIBO treatment failed - the peer-reviewed strains matter
reading that Impossible Burger has 18 engineered ingredients vs a Pat LaFrieda 80/20 patty's single one - I went back to real beef and my cholesterol actually improved
discovering Four Sigmatic mushroom coffee at $17.99/bag - the chaga and lion's mane blend actually sharpened my focus by week three, my Apple Watch stress score confirmed it
those HelloFresh meals that claimed '30 minute prep' but actually took 55 minutes with all the measuring and chopping - cancelled after week 2
subscribing to Trade Coffee at $15.99/bag delivered every 2 weeks - haven't thought about coffee shopping in 18 months
that $9.99 Sweetgreen salad that went brown and slimy 2 days before the use-by date - wasted money and still had to figure out dinner
that Motor City frozen pizza from Costco that's legitimately good and ready in 12 minutes - my Tuesday night savior for $5.49
paying the $9.99 Instacart Priority surge on Super Bowl Sunday 2023 to skip a 2-hour Trader Joe's parking loop - best ten dollars I spent that month
signing up for Daily Harvest smoothies at $8.99 per cup - two minutes from freezer to blender to out the door, my 7am brain does not have to think
when Factor meals arrived pre-cooked and I realized I'd saved four hours of weeknight cooking for $79/week - the microwave-only format is a cheat code for busy parents
that Banza chickpea pasta with 25g protein that cooks in 8 minutes - my no-thought Tuesday dinner since 2022, pairs with jarred sauce and calls it done
auto-shipping Huel Black Edition every four weeks at $89 - breakfast solved with zero decision fatigue, scoop-shake-drink by 7:02am
when Amazon Fresh started same-day delivery in my zip code in 2022 and I stopped going to Safeway entirely - 14 months without setting foot in a grocery store
that Trader Joe's orange chicken frozen bag microwave hack - 4 minutes, restaurant-quality for $4.99, enough for two, no pan to scrub
subscribing to Butcher Box at $169/box and not thinking about meat quality for three months at a time - I pull a chicken out of the freezer at 5pm and dinner is sorted
the HelloFresh cancellation I regretted immediately when Monday at 6:45pm hit and I stared blankly at an empty fridge with three hungry kids
finding RXBar's Amazon Subscribe & Save at 15% off - breakfast at my desk, no prep, solved for 2024 with a click and a recurring charge
that Wonder delivery truck in NYC - I tap a button, a Bobby Flay-quality meal arrives in twelve minutes at $24, my Tuesday night doesn't need a stove
using DoorDash DashPass at $9.99/month and saving $47 in delivery fees across eight weeknight dinners in March - paid for itself by week two
when Thrive Market's auto-refill cut my 'running out of oat milk' stress to zero for six months straight - set it and never think about it again
that Trader Joe's pre-chopped mirepoix at $2.99 - my weekly Sunday soup takes 20 minutes instead of 45, I'll pay the markup for my knife time
that Kettle & Fire bone broth tetra pack at $7.99 - five minutes from pantry to sick-day dinner, the collagen in the sip replaces the delivery fee from a deli
my Crock-Pot Express 8qt paid itself off in two months when I stopped ordering DoorDash on work-from-home Mondays - dump, press, dinner
the Walmart+ subscription at $98/year that gave me free grocery delivery and saved my sanity through the 2022 baby year - never setting foot in a store with a newborn saved my marriage
cancelling Freshly after Nestle took over in 2020 - sodium went from 480 to 890mg per meal, the texture tanked, and the same price bought the same plastic tray
buying Costco's $4.99 rotisserie chicken every Sunday and stripping it into four weeknight meals - my lazy-efficient 2019 routine that still runs today
that Gorillas delivery app in Brooklyn that promised 10-minute groceries and died in 2022 - I still haven't replaced the speed even with Uber Grocery
that Yummly app that texts me when my usual ingredients go on sale at Kroger - I don't even have to plan the week, the phone does it while I'm in meetings
serving that $42 Frantoia olive oil at my dinner party in November and watching guests actually pause mid-bite to ask about it
being mortified when my 'exclusive' Manchego from Murray's turned out to be the same SKU as the $8.99 Costco wheel
discovering Counter Culture's Hologram blend at $18/12oz from a roaster who visits their Ethiopian farms annually - the real deal
that Primal Kitchen sauce line that Gwyneth hawked - literally just Hampton Creek mayo with turmeric for $11.99, felt scammed
buying the Erewhon Hailey Bieber Strawberry Glaze smoothie at $19 and posting it to my story - 400 likes and three DMs asking where I was, would do it again tomorrow
bringing the Flamingo Estate Italian herbs jar to the Pilates girls' dinner - $48 for dried basil but the label photographs like a Getty museum piece
serving La Colombe Draft Latte cans at my morning book club and watching Nicole actually pull her phone out to Google the brand - mission accomplished
that Caraway cookware set in cream at $545 - opened the box on camera for my TikTok, sponsored skillet posts started rolling in by week three
when I dropped $38 on a Graza Drizzle bottle at Erewhon and my Italian mother-in-law asked why I'd buy 'designer oil' - the green squeeze bottle is the whole point, not the olive
that Great Jones Dutch oven in Broccoli green sitting on my range at $195 - the kitchen shoots went from 12 likes to 340 once it was in frame
picking up the Omsom Thai Larb starter kit at $12 from the Whole Foods endcap and acting like I'd found it on a Bangkok trip - it photographs as research, looks like curation
serving Ghia aperitif at my 'sober curious' dinner party in 2023 - the bottle alone cost $38, more than my entire Pellegrino case, and it photographed like Sardinian sunset
that Loewe home candle tin from the Ssense sale - smells like $300 and sits permanently on my kitchen counter for Reels - not even the food is the main character anymore
when I hosted a Moon Juice cocktail hour with the $48 Tulsi Tea tincture - the girls still reference it two years later when they see anything cloudy-blue in a bottle
dropping $72 on a Maman cookbook at Equinox and leaving it on the coffee table with a ribbon as a bookmark - nobody's opened it, everyone's mentioned it
my Fellow Stagg EKG electric kettle in matte black at $195 - every Reel I film features it in frame, the spout pour is a whole narrative beat
bringing a bottle of Avaline rosé (Cameron Diaz's wine) to a dinner party and the host asking for the Instagram handle mid-pour - she didn't ask about the food
that $64 Fishwife tinned fish gift box from Goldbelly for my MIL's birthday - Olympia Provisions salami felt basic next to the smoked trout in elderflower tomato oil
posting my De Buyer carbon steel pan with the eggs mid-flip - a food blogger with 89K followers reposted it to her stories, my own followers doubled that week
that Maude Vibes orange wine at Gjelina - $22 a glass but I tagged them on Instagram and they reposted it to their main, worth the expense report explanation
picking Courage & Craft cabernet from Whole Foods' curated shelf at $28 - the label is genuine dining room art and I kept the empty bottle as a vase
buying Ayesha Curry's Homemade chicken stock at Target just to see what her name tasted like - $6.99 for a celebrity endorsement that went in a soup
that Erewhon skin-bar protein smoothie at $22 in the Venice parking lot - my morning ritual that justifies my 90291 zip code to my parents in Ohio
that Whispering Angel rosé poolside at the Hamptons share house - nobody actually drinks it anymore but the bottle is still the shot we all reach for
posting the Jeni's Splendid Brambleberry Crisp pint in a blue-speckled ceramic bowl - 600 likes on my grid vs 40 for a generic Ben & Jerry's flavor last month
my Heath Ceramics bud vase beside a Farmhouse Kitchen Thai chili crunch jar - kitchen staging for the client shoot made the jar a $14 purchase that feels like decor
that Just Water 'eco-friendly' bottle from Jaden Smith that's 82% paper but still has a plastic lining and cap - greenwashing 101
finding Fillmore Container's refill station in my neighborhood where I can bring my own jars for Dr. Bronner's at $6.50/lb
calling out Shell's 'Drive Carbon Neutral' campaign on Twitter for being just offsets while they drill in the Arctic - got 4K retweets
when Grove Collaborative actually switched to 100% recycled bottles after the petition I signed - took 2 years but real change
reading that Oatly's carbon footprint claim got challenged by the UK ASA in 2022 - turns out they'd self-scored their own math, the 73% less claim wasn't independent
when I learned Poland Spring draws aquifers shared with drought-stricken Maine towns - switched to Boxed Water and put a filter on my tap that same afternoon
that Patagonia Provisions salmon at $9.99/pouch is actually regenerative and the label shows the specific river catchment - finally a brand that shows the math
realizing my Keurig K-Cup habit landfills 1,200 pods a year per household - switched to a French press and Atlas Coffee Club whole bean in January 2023
when I read the Dasani bottle is only 17% recycled PET - the other 83% is virgin plastic from a Gulf refinery the marketing conveniently forgets to mention
that Beyond Meat patty uses 99% less water than beef but still comes in a plastic tray with a plastic film over a plastic liner - two steps forward, one step back
the rage of finding out Ben & Jerry's Save Our Swirled climate-message flavor got discontinued in 2019 because it wasn't selling - activism cancelled by logistics
when Nestle's CEO said water isn't a human right in a 2013 documentary - I still don't buy any Nestle brand 11 years later, Poland Spring Perrier Nescafe all banned
using Whole Foods bulk bins with my own Weck jars for four years - saved 3,400 plastic bags per my spreadsheet tally, the cashiers now know my tare weights by heart
subscribing to Misfits Market in 2020 for ugly produce at 40% off grocery prices while cutting food waste - my carbon math says I save 86lbs of CO2 a month
when Starbucks claimed they'd eliminate plastic straws but replaced them with bigger plastic lids that use more plastic per unit - optics over outcomes
reading the Cheerios box that says 'made with 100% whole grain oats' while the bag inside is virgin LDPE film that can't be curbside recycled - the irony is plastic
that Who Gives A Crap toilet paper at $48 for 48 rolls - 100% recycled, bamboo, plastic-free wrap, 50% of profits to sanitation projects - subscription since 2019
when Coca-Cola got named the #1 plastic polluter globally for the fifth year running in 2023 - their house-banned status is permanent at our fridge
the compostable Repurpose cups I bring to every family BBQ while my Aunt Debbie complains about the $0.25-per-cup markup - she hasn't swallowed a microplastic argument yet
reading that Tyson had 12 million gallons of wastewater violations in 2022 - switched to ButcherBox for actual sourcing accountability, the chicken costs more and I sleep better
learning Trader Joe's almond milk tetra paks are only technically recyclable and 80% still end up in landfills because local MRFs can't process them - switched to homemade
that Loop refillable container program at Kroger in 2021 that quietly died by 2023 - real circular packaging stays unprofitable while the plastic keeps flowing
when Impossible Foods got the UN Environmental Award in 2019 but their soy still comes from deforested Brazilian Cerrado land via ADM and Bunge
finding Bee's Wrap at $19 for a 3-pack replaced six months of cling film in my kitchen - plastic kitchen waste down 70% per my trash-bag audit
that Allbirds Net Zero campaign where they literally said 'we will not become net zero through offsets' - bought two pairs the same week to reward the honesty
making that TikTok baked feta pasta in March 2021 when it had 200M views - actually lived up to the hype and I posted my version
waiting 45 minutes for that Tiger Sugar boba when they opened in my city - $7.50 for a drink that was honestly just okay
being the first one to find Fly By Jing chili crisp before it blew up - now I'm the hot sauce plug in my friend group
my Instagram story of that Cha Cha Matcha latte against the pink wall got 287 likes - more than my engagement photos
buying Olipop Vintage Cola the week it went viral on TikTok in May 2023 - now I've got four flavors chilling in my fridge at all times for the Reels B-roll
that Stanley Quencher 40oz in blush pink I camped outside Target for 3 hours to grab - sold it for $125 on Poshmark a month later when the hype peaked
making the cottage-cheese ice cream TikTok recipe 40 times last summer - Hailey Bieber couldn't have marketed it better if she'd tried, Good Culture was sold out for weeks
that whipped Dalgona coffee from March 2020 that got 50M TikTok views - my hands ached from whisking for 8 minutes but my story slayed with 400+ views
trying the Gym Class peanut butter brownie protein powder after Alix Earle posted it on her story - $49 for 25 servings, worth every scoop if only for the aesthetic
when everyone went feral for that Costco Kirkland chocolate chip cookie dough in December 2023 - bought four tubs to freeze before the Reddit stockpilers cleared the shelves
ordering the Poppi prebiotic soda 12-pack on Amazon after the Super Bowl ad aired in February 2024 - my gut health era has a flavor palette and an AOR
finding Chamoy pickles viral on TikTok in 2022 and ordering $38 of them to my apartment just to film the cut-reveal - the squelch sound got 60K views
that Celsius lineup on my desk after Emma Chamberlain said she drinks them in her YouTube vlog - swapped my LaCroix habit at $2.50/can in one week flat
trying Liquid Death sparkling water because the cans 'slap' per TikTok - the marketing won me over before the first sip and I posted a crushed can to my story
that green goddess salad from Baked by Melissa that broke TikTok in summer 2022 - made it for three weeks straight, still my go-to cookout bring
buying Graza Drizzle olive oil because every food creator on my For You page had it on their counter in February 2023 - the green squeeze bottle is the content
when Magnolia Bakery banana pudding went viral again in 2021 - waited 45 minutes in SoHo at 11pm just to film the scoop shot for my story in slo-mo
that Caraway pan in Perracotta terra cotta after the Bobbi Brown endorsement - hung it on my wall next to the cast iron instead of using it, the aesthetic is the point
ordering the Prime hydration ice-pop flavor for my 11-year-old after Logan Paul's marketing hit TikTok - it sat in the fridge for six months untouched after the YouTube drama broke
buying Topo Chico Hard Seltzer the week it launched in 2021 because Eater said it was the 'next White Claw' - the hashtag played out by Memorial Day weekend
that cucumber salad moment on TikTok in summer 2024 - dug out the Benriner mandoline and made the Logan's Roadhouse version eight times in two weeks
trying Hot Ones Last Dab hot sauce after a Sean Evans episode with Gordon Ramsay - cried through the first wing and posted the video, got 12K views on my reel
buying the Lululemon Align set and a Celsius cherry limeade for the 'fit check' Reel - the Celsius was the entire point of the content, the workout was incidental
that Fishwife tinned sardines moment when Carla Music posted them on Bon Appetit in 2022 - $15 a tin feels insane until you plate them on sourdough and watch the likes roll in
ordering Chamberlain Coffee cold brew on Amazon because Emma Chamberlain's brand identity matched my vibe in June 2023 - my fridge is a mood board now, not a fridge
